reasoning: The ECB is currently undergoing Quantitative Tightening, allowing APP and PEPP holdings to predictably run off without reinvestment. This leads to a persistent and mechanical decline in central bank assets of tens of billions of euros per month. Over the subsequent months (expected through early 2026), this downward trend heavily outweighs standard weekly fluctuations in liquidity-providing operations, making any net increase in the aggregate balance sheet relative to October 2025 highly unlikely unless a sudden, severe financial crisis forces mass liquidity injections.
